info.title: PostHog Python SDK
info.description: Integrate PostHog into any python application.

classes.title: PostHog
classes.description: This is the SDK reference for the PostHog Python SDK. You can learn more about example usage in the [Python SDK documentation](/docs/libraries/python). You can also follow [Flask](/docs/libraries/flask) and [Django](/docs/libraries/django) guides to integrate PostHog into your project.  For long-running applications, create one client during application startup and reuse it for the lifetime of the process. This keeps background queues predictable and makes shutdown flushing straightforward. Multiple clients are still supported for intentional multi-project or multi-host setups.

functions.title: Client
functions.description: Initialize a new PostHog client instance.

params.name: project_api_key
params.description: PostHog project API key/token.

params.name: host
params.description: PostHog host. Defaults to the US ingestion endpoint when not         set. App hosts such as ``https://us.posthog.com`` are mapped to         the corresponding ingestion host.

params.name: debug
params.description: Enable verbose SDK logging and re-raise errors from public         API methods.

params.name: max_queue_size
params.description: Maximum number of events buffered before upload.

params.name: send
params.description: If False, queueing succeeds but events are not sent.

params.name: on_error
params.description: Optional callback invoked by background consumers when an         upload fails.

params.name: flush_at
params.description: Number of queued events that triggers a batch upload.

params.name: flush_interval
params.description: Maximum seconds a background consumer waits before         flushing a partial batch.

params.name: gzip
params.description: Whether to gzip event upload payloads.

params.name: max_retries
params.description: Number of upload retries for background consumers.

params.name: sync_mode
params.description: If True, send each event synchronously instead of using         background worker threads.

params.name: timeout
params.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds for event uploads.

params.name: thread
params.description: Number of background consumer threads.

params.name: poll_interval
params.description: Seconds between local feature flag definition refreshes.

params.name: personal_api_key
params.description: Deprecated alias for ``secret_key``. Still honored         for backwards compatibility; prefer ``secret_key``, which also         accepts a Project Secret API Key.

params.name: disabled
params.description: If True, disable captures and API requests. Useful in tests.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable server-side GeoIP enrichment.         Defaults to True.

params.name: is_server
params.description: Whether events are emitted from a server-side runtime.         Defaults to True; set to False when using the SDK as a client/CLI         so the device OS is attributed to the person normally.

params.name: historical_migration
params.description: Mark events as historical migration imports.

params.name: feature_flags_request_timeout_seconds
params.description: Timeout in seconds for feature         flag and remote config requests.

params.name: feature_flags_request_max_retries
params.description: Number of retries for feature flag         requests after network, transport, or timeout failures. Defaults         to 1. Set to 0 to disable retries.

params.name: super_properties
params.description: Properties merged into every captured event.

params.name: enable_exception_autocapture
params.description: Automatically capture uncaught         exceptions.

params.name: log_captured_exceptions
params.description: Also log exceptions captured by error         tracking.

params.name: project_root
params.description: Root path used to determine in-app stack frames for         captured exceptions. Defaults to the current working directory.

params.name: privacy_mode
params.description: For AI observability, capture usage metadata without         prompt inputs or outputs.

params.name: before_send
params.description: Optional callback that can modify or drop events before         upload. Return ``None`` to drop an event.

params.name: flag_fallback_cache_url
params.description: Optional feature flag fallback cache URL,         such as ``memory://local/?ttl=300&size=10000`` or a Redis URL.

params.name: enable_local_evaluation
params.description: Whether to poll feature flag definitions for         local evaluation when a personal API key is configured.

params.name: flag_definition_cache_provider
params.description: Optional external cache provider for         sharing feature flag definitions across workers.

params.name: capture_exception_code_variables
params.description: Capture local variable values on         exception stack frames.

params.name: code_variables_mask_patterns
params.description: Variable-name patterns to mask when         capturing code variables.

params.name: code_variables_ignore_patterns
params.description: Variable-name patterns to omit when         capturing code variables.

params.name: code_variables_mask_url_credentials
params.description: Scrub credentials embedded in         URLs/DSNs (e.g. ``user:pass@host``) from captured code variables,         regardless of the surrounding variable name. Defaults to True.

params.name: code_variables_detect_secrets
params.description: Last-resort entropy-based detection that         redacts high-entropy secret-looking values (API keys, tokens, strong         passwords) sitting in innocuously-named variables, after the name and         URL checks. Skips structured ids (UUIDs, ObjectIds, hashes). Defaults         to True.

params.name: in_app_modules
params.description: Module/package prefixes treated as in-app frames in         captured exceptions.

params.name: enable_exception_autocapture_rate_limiting
params.description: Rate limit         autocaptured exceptions client-side with a token bucket per         exception type. Disabled by default.

params.name: exception_autocapture_bucket_size
params.description: Maximum burst of autocaptured         exceptions allowed per exception type (token bucket size,         clamped to 0-100).

params.name: exception_autocapture_refill_rate
params.description: Tokens restored per refill         interval for each exception type's bucket.

params.name: exception_autocapture_refill_interval_seconds
params.description: Seconds between         token refills for autocaptured exception rate limiting.

params.name: capture_mode
params.description: Capture wire protocol to use. Defaults to         ``CaptureMode.V0`` (legacy ``/batch/``). Set ``CaptureMode.V1``         (or pass the string ``"v1"``) to opt into         ``/i/v1/analytics/events``. When omitted, the         ``POSTHOG_CAPTURE_MODE`` env var is consulted, then ``V0``.

params.name: capture_compression
params.description: Request-body compression for capture-v1 uploads         (ignored in V0, which uses ``gzip``). ``CaptureCompression.GZIP``         or ``DEFLATE`` (or the strings ``"gzip"``/``"deflate"``). When         omitted, the ``POSTHOG_CAPTURE_COMPRESSION`` env var is consulted,         then the legacy ``gzip`` flag, then no compression.

params.name: secret_key
params.description: A Personal API Key or Project Secret API Key, used to         authenticate local feature flag evaluation, remote config         payloads, and decrypted flag payloads. Example::              posthog.Client(project_api_key, secret_key="phx_...")

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import Posthog

posthog = Posthog('<ph_project_api_key>', host='<ph_app_host>')

functions.title: alias
functions.description: Create an alias between two distinct IDs.

params.name: previous_id
params.description: The previous distinct ID.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The new distinct ID to alias to.

params.name: timestamp
params.description: The timestamp of the event.

params.name: uuid
params.description: A unique identifier for the event. If provided, it must be a         valid UUID string or uuid.UUID instance; invalid values are         ignored and replaced with a newly generated UUID.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP for this event.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code: posthog.alias(previous_id='distinct_id', distinct_id='alias_id')

functions.title: capture
functions.description: Captures an event manually. [Learn about capture best practices](https://posthog.com/docs/product-analytics/capture-events)

params.name: event
params.description: The event name to capture.

examples.name: Anonymous event
examples.code:
# Anonymous event
posthog.capture('some-anon-event')

examples.name: Context usage
examples.code:
# Context usage
from posthog import identify_context, new_context
with new_context():
    identify_context('distinct_id_of_the_user')
    posthog.capture('user_signed_up')
    posthog.capture('user_logged_in')
    posthog.capture('some-custom-action', distinct_id='distinct_id_of_the_user')

examples.name: Set event properties
Page view event
examples.code:
# Page view event
posthog.capture('$pageview', distinct_id="distinct_id_of_the_user", properties={'$current_url': 'https://example.com'})

functions.title: capture_exception
functions.description: Capture an exception for error tracking.  When OpenTelemetry is installed and a valid span is active, its trace and span IDs are added as ``$trace_id`` and ``$span_id`` event properties.

params.name: exception
params.description: The exception to capture.

examples.name: Example 1

functions.title: evaluate_flags
functions.description: Evaluate all feature flags for a user in a single call and return a :class:`FeatureFlagEvaluations` snapshot. Branch on ``.is_enabled()`` / ``.get_flag()`` and pass the same snapshot to :meth:`capture` via the ``flags`` option so events carry the exact flag values the code branched on.  Prefer this over repeated ``get_feature_flag()`` calls and over ``capture(send_feature_flags=True)`` — it consolidates flag evaluation into a single ``/flags`` request per incoming request.  Local evaluation is transparent: when the poller resolves a flag, the snapshot's ``$feature_flag_called`` events are tagged ``locally_evaluated=True`` and reason ``"Evaluated locally"``.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID. If ``None``, falls back to the         context distinct_id. If still unresolvable, returns an empty snapshot.

params.name: groups
params.description: Mapping of group type to group key.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties to use for evaluation.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties keyed by group type.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: If True, never fall back to remote evaluation —         flags that can't be evaluated locally are simply omitted from the snapshot.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup.

params.name: flag_keys
params.description: Optional list of flag keys to scope the underlying ``/flags``         request to a subset.

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override. If not provided, falls back to the         context device_id (which may be set via tracing headers). Used by         experience-continuity flags to match users across distinct_id changes.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
flags = posthog.evaluate_flags(
    "user_123",
    person_properties={"plan": "enterprise"},
)
if flags.is_enabled("new-dashboard"):
    render_new_dashboard()
posthog.capture("page_viewed", distinct_id="user_123", flags=flags)

functions.title: feature_enabled
functions.description: Check if a feature flag is enabled for a user.

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The distinct ID of the user.

params.name: groups
params.description: A dictionary of group information.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: A dictionary of person properties.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: A dictionary of group properties.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to only evaluate locally.

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Whether to send feature flag events.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP for this request.

params.name: device_id
params.description: The device ID for this request.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
is_my_flag_enabled = posthog.feature_enabled('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')
if is_my_flag_enabled:
    # Do something differently for this user
    # Optional: fetch the payload
    matched_flag_payload = posthog.get_feature_flag_payload('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')

functions.title: feature_flag_definitions
functions.description: Return feature flag definitions loaded for local evaluation.  Returns:     The currently loaded feature flag definitions, or ``None`` before     local evaluation has loaded definitions.

functions.title: flush
functions.description: Force a flush from the internal queue to the server. Do not use directly, call `shutdown()` instead.

params.name: timeout_seconds
params.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the queue to flush.         Defaults to 10 seconds. Pass ``None`` to wait indefinitely.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
posthog.capture('event_name')
posthog.flush()  # Ensures the event is sent immediately

functions.title: get_feature_flag
functions.description: Get multivariate feature flag value for a user.

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The distinct ID of the user.

params.name: groups
params.description: A dictionary of group information.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: A dictionary of person properties.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: A dictionary of group properties.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to only evaluate locally.

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Whether to send feature flag events.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP for this request.

params.name: device_id
params.description: The device ID for this request.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
enabled_variant = posthog.get_feature_flag('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')
if enabled_variant == 'variant-key': # replace 'variant-key' with the key of your variant
    # Do something differently for this user
    # Optional: fetch the payload
    matched_flag_payload = posthog.get_feature_flag_payload('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')

functions.title: get_feature_flag_payload
functions.description: Get the payload for a feature flag.

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The distinct ID of the user.

params.name: match_value
params.description: The specific flag value to get payload for.

params.name: groups
params.description: A dictionary of group information.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: A dictionary of person properties.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: A dictionary of group properties.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to only evaluate locally.

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Deprecated. Use get_feature_flag() instead if you need events.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP for this request.

params.name: device_id
params.description: The device ID for this request.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
is_my_flag_enabled = posthog.feature_enabled('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')

if is_my_flag_enabled:
    # Do something differently for this user
    # Optional: fetch the payload
    matched_flag_payload = posthog.get_feature_flag_payload('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')

functions.title: get_feature_flag_result
functions.description: Get a FeatureFlagResult object which contains the flag result and payload for a key by evaluating locally or remotely depending on whether local evaluation is enabled and the flag can be locally evaluated. This also captures the `$feature_flag_called` event unless `send_feature_flag_events` is `False`.

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The distinct ID of the user.

params.name: groups
params.description: A dictionary of group information.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: A dictionary of person properties.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: A dictionary of group properties.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to only evaluate locally.

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Whether to send feature flag events.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP for this request.

params.name: device_id
params.description: The device ID for this request.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
flag_result = posthog.get_feature_flag_result('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')
if flag_result and flag_result.get_value() == 'variant-key':
    # Do something differently for this user
    # Optional: fetch the payload
    matched_flag_payload = flag_result.payload

functions.title: new_context
functions.description: Create a new context for managing shared state. Learn more about [contexts](/docs/libraries/python#contexts).

params.name: fresh
params.description: Whether to create a fresh context that doesn't inherit from parent.

params.name: capture_exceptions
params.description: Whether to automatically capture exceptions in this context. If omitted, defaults to this client's exception autocapture setting.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
with client.new_context():
    client.identify_context('<distinct_id>')
    client.capture('event_name')

params.name: fresh

params.name: capture_exceptions
params.description: Whether to automatically capture exceptions in this context. If omitted, defaults to this client's exception autocapture setting.

examples.name: Example 1

functions.description: Add a tag to the current context.

params.name: name
params.description: The tag key.

params.name: value
params.description: The tag value.

classes.title: PostHog Module Functions
classes.description: Global functions available in the PostHog module

functions.title: alias
functions.description: Associate user behaviour before and after they e.g. register, login, or perform some other identifying action.
functions.details: To marry up whatever a user does before they sign up or log in with what they do after you need to make an alias call. This will allow you to answer questions like "Which marketing channels leads to users churning after a month?" or "What do users do on our website before signing up?". Particularly useful for associating user behaviour before and after they e.g. register, login, or perform some other identifying action.

params.name: previous_id
params.description: The unique ID of the user before

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The current unique id

params.name: timestamp
params.description: Optional timestamp for the event

params.name: uuid
params.description: Optional UUID for the event

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup

examples.name: Alias user
examples.code:
# Alias user
from posthog import alias
alias(previous_id='distinct_id', distinct_id='alias_id')

functions.title: capture
functions.description: Capture anything a user does within your system.
functions.details: Capture allows you to capture anything a user does within your system, which you can later use in PostHog to find patterns in usage, work out which features to improve or where people are giving up. A capture call requires an event name to specify the event. We recommend using [verb] [noun], like `movie played` or `movie updated` to easily identify what your events mean later on. Capture takes a number of optional arguments, which are defined by the `OptionalCaptureArgs` type.

params.name: event
params.description: The event name to specify the event     **kwargs: Optional arguments including:

examples.name: Context and capture usage
examples.code:
# Context and capture usage
from posthog import new_context, identify_context, tag_context, capture
# Enter a new context (e.g. a request/response cycle, an instance of a background job, etc)
with new_context():
    # Associate this context with some user, by distinct_id
    identify_context('some user')

    # Capture an event, associated with the context-level distinct ID ('some user')
    capture('movie started')

    # Capture an event associated with some other user (overriding the context-level distinct ID)
    capture('movie joined', distinct_id='some-other-user')

    # Capture an event with some properties
    capture('movie played', properties={'movie_id': '123', 'category': 'romcom'})

    # Capture an event with some properties
    capture('purchase', properties={'product_id': '123', 'category': 'romcom'})
    # Capture an event with some associated group
    capture('purchase', groups={'company': 'id:5'})

    # Adding a tag to the current context will cause it to appear on all subsequent events
    tag_context('some-tag', 'some-value')

    capture('another-event') # Will be captured with `'some-tag': 'some-value'` in the properties dict

examples.name: Set event properties

functions.title: capture_exception
functions.description: Capture exceptions that happen in your code.
functions.details: Capture exception is idempotent - if it is called twice with the same exception instance, only a occurrence will be tracked in posthog. This is because, generally, contexts will cause exceptions to be captured automatically. However, to ensure you track an exception, if you catch and do not re-raise it, capturing it manually is recommended, unless you are certain it will have crossed a context boundary (e.g. by existing a `with posthog.new_context():` block already). If the passed exception was raised and caught, the captured stack trace will consist of every frame between where the exception was raised and the point at which it is captured (the "traceback"). If the passed exception was never raised, e.g. if you call `posthog.capture_exception(ValueError("Some Error"))`, the stack trace captured will be the full stack trace at the moment the exception was captured. Note that heavy use of contexts will lead to truncated stack traces, as the exception will be captured by the context entered most recently, which may not be the point you catch the exception for the final time in your code. It's recommended to use contexts sparingly, for this reason. `capture_exception` takes the same set of optional arguments as `capture`.

params.name: exception
params.description: The exception to capture. If not provided, the current exception is captured via `sys.exc_info()`     **kwargs: Optional capture arguments including distinct_id, properties,         timestamp, uuid, groups, flags, send_feature_flags, and disable_geoip.

examples.name: Capture exception

functions.title: evaluate_flags
functions.description: Evaluate all feature flags for a user in a single call and return a :class:`FeatureFlagEvaluations` snapshot. Branch on ``.is_enabled()`` / ``.get_flag()`` and pass the same snapshot to ``capture()`` via the ``flags`` option so events carry the exact flag values the code branched on.  Prefer this over repeated ``get_feature_flag()`` calls and over ``capture(send_feature_flags=True)`` — it consolidates flag evaluation into a single ``/flags`` request per incoming request.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID. If ``None``, falls back to the context         distinct_id. If still unresolvable, returns an empty snapshot.

params.name: groups
params.description: Mapping of group type to group key.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties to use for evaluation.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties keyed by group type.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: If ``True``, never fall back to remote evaluation.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup.

params.name: flag_keys
params.description: Optional list of flag keys. When provided, only these flags are         evaluated — the underlying ``/flags`` request asks the server for just         this subset, which makes the response smaller and the request cheaper.         Use this when you only need a handful of flags out of many.

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override. If not provided, falls back to the         context device_id (which may be set via tracing headers). Used by         experience-continuity flags to match users across distinct_id changes.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import evaluate_flags, capture
flags = evaluate_flags("user_123", person_properties={"plan": "enterprise"})
if flags.is_enabled("new-dashboard"):
    render_new_dashboard()
capture("page_viewed", distinct_id="user_123", flags=flags)

functions.title: feature_enabled
functions.description: Use feature flags to enable or disable features for users.
functions.details: You can call `posthog.load_feature_flags()` before to make sure you're not doing unexpected requests.

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID

params.name: groups
params.description: Groups mapping

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to evaluate only locally

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Whether to send feature flag events

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override for experience-continuity flags

examples.name: Boolean feature flag
examples.code:
# Boolean feature flag
from posthog import feature_enabled, get_feature_flag_payload
is_my_flag_enabled = feature_enabled('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')
if is_my_flag_enabled:
    matched_flag_payload = get_feature_flag_payload('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')

functions.title: feature_flag_definitions
functions.description: Returns loaded feature flags.
functions.details: Returns loaded feature flags, if any. Helpful for debugging what flag information you have loaded.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import feature_flag_definitions
definitions = feature_flag_definitions()

functions.title: flush
functions.description: Tell the client to flush all queued events.

params.name: timeout_seconds
params.description: Maximum seconds to wait for the queue to flush.         Defaults to 10 seconds. Pass ``None`` to wait indefinitely.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import flush
flush()

functions.title: get_all_flags
functions.description: Get all flags for a given user.
functions.details: Flags are key-value pairs where the key is the flag key and the value is the flag variant, or True, or False.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID

params.name: groups
params.description: Groups mapping

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to evaluate only locally

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override for experience-continuity flags

params.name: flag_keys_to_evaluate
params.description: Optional list of flag keys to evaluate (evaluates all if None)

examples.name: All flags for user
examples.code:
# All flags for user
from posthog import get_all_flags
get_all_flags('distinct_id_of_your_user')

functions.title: get_all_flags_and_payloads
functions.description: Get all feature flag values and payloads for a user.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID.

params.name: groups
params.description: Mapping of group type to group key.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties to use for evaluation.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties keyed by group type.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to evaluate only locally.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup.

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override for experience-continuity flags.

params.name: flag_keys_to_evaluate
params.description: Optional list of flag keys to evaluate. Evaluates         all flags when omitted.

functions.title: get_feature_flag
functions.description: Get feature flag variant for users. Used with experiments.
functions.details: `groups` are a mapping from group type to group key. So, if you have a group type of "organization" and a group key of "5", you would pass groups={"organization": "5"}. `group_properties` take the format: { group_type_name: { group_properties } }. So, for example, if you have the group type "organization" and the group key "5", with the properties name, and employee count, you'll send these as: group_properties={"organization": {"name": "PostHog", "employees": 11}}.

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID

params.name: groups
params.description: Groups mapping from group type to group key

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties in format { group_type_name: { group_properties } }

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to evaluate only locally

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Whether to send feature flag events

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override for experience-continuity flags

examples.name: Multivariate feature flag
examples.code:
# Multivariate feature flag
from posthog import get_feature_flag, get_feature_flag_payload
enabled_variant = get_feature_flag('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')
if enabled_variant == 'variant-key':
    matched_flag_payload = get_feature_flag_payload('flag-key', 'distinct_id_of_your_user')

functions.title: get_feature_flag_payload
functions.description: Get the payload associated with a feature flag value.  Deprecated for new code. Prefer ``evaluate_flags()`` and ``flags.get_flag_payload(key)`` so flag evaluation happens once per request.

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID.

params.name: match_value
params.description: Optional flag value to use when selecting a payload.

params.name: groups
params.description: Mapping of group type to group key.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties to use for evaluation.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties keyed by group type.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to evaluate only locally.

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Whether to send a $feature_flag_called event.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup.

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override for experience-continuity flags.

functions.title: get_feature_flag_result
functions.description: Get a FeatureFlagResult object which contains the flag result and payload.  This method evaluates a feature flag and returns a FeatureFlagResult object containing: - enabled: Whether the flag is enabled - variant: The variant value if the flag has variants - payload: The payload associated with the flag (automatically deserialized from JSON) - key: The flag key - reason: Why the flag was enabled/disabled

params.name: key
params.description: The feature flag key.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The user's distinct ID.

params.name: groups
params.description: Mapping of group type to group key.

params.name: person_properties
params.description: Person properties to use for evaluation.

params.name: group_properties
params.description: Group properties keyed by group type.

params.name: only_evaluate_locally
params.description: Whether to evaluate only locally.

params.name: send_feature_flag_events
params.description: Whether to send a $feature_flag_called event.

params.name: disable_geoip
params.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookup.

params.name: device_id
params.description: Optional device ID override for experience-continuity flags.

functions.title: get_remote_config_payload
functions.description: Get the payload for a remote config feature flag.

params.name: key
params.description: The key of the feature flag

functions.title: get_tags
functions.description: Get all tags from the current context.  Returns:     Dict of all tags in the current context

functions.description: Set properties on a group.

params.name: group_type
params.description: Type of your group

params.name: group_key
params.description: Unique identifier of the group

params.name: properties
params.description: Properties to set on the group

params.name: timestamp
params.description: Optional timestamp for the event

params.name: uuid
params.description: Optional UUID for the event

params.name: disable_geoip

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: Optional distinct ID of the user performing the action

functions.title: identify_context
functions.description: Identify the current context with a distinct ID.

params.name: distinct_id
params.description: The distinct ID to associate with the current context and its children

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import identify_context
identify_context("user_123")

functions.title: join
functions.description: Block program until the client clears the queue. Used during program shutdown. You should use `shutdown()` directly in most cases.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import join
join()

functions.title: load_feature_flags
functions.description: Load feature flag definitions from PostHog.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import load_feature_flags
load_feature_flags()

functions.title: new_context
functions.description: Create a new context scope that will be active for the duration of the with block.

params.name: fresh
params.description: Whether to start with a fresh context (default: False)

params.name: capture_exceptions
params.description: Whether to capture exceptions raised within the context. If omitted, defaults to the relevant client's exception autocapture setting.

params.name: client
params.description: Optional Posthog client instance to use for this context (default: None)

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import new_context, tag, capture
with new_context():
    tag("request_id", "123")
    capture("event_name", properties={"property": "value"})

functions.title: scoped
functions.description: Decorator that creates a new context for the function.

params.name: fresh
params.description: Whether to start with a fresh context (default: False)

params.name: capture_exceptions
params.description: Whether to capture and track exceptions with posthog error tracking. If omitted, defaults to the global exception autocapture setting.

examples.name: Example 1

functions.title: set
functions.description: Set properties on a user record.
functions.details: This will overwrite previous people property values. Generally operates similar to `capture`, with distinct_id being an optional argument, defaulting to the current context's distinct ID. If there is no context-level distinct ID, and no override distinct_id is passed, this function will do nothing. Context tags are folded into $set properties, so tagging the current context and then calling `set` will cause those tags to be set on the user (unlike capture, which causes them to just be set on the event).

examples.name: Set person properties
examples.code:
# Set person properties
from posthog import set
set(distinct_id='distinct_id', properties={'name': 'Max Hedgehog'})

functions.title: set_capture_exception_code_variables_context
functions.description: Override code-variable capture for exceptions in the current context.

params.name: enabled
params.description: Whether exceptions captured in this context should include local         variable values from stack frames.

functions.title: set_code_variables_detect_secrets_context
functions.description: Whether to apply entropy-based secret detection as a last-resort redaction of high-entropy values (API keys, tokens, strong passwords) in captured code variables for the current context.

functions.title: set_code_variables_ignore_patterns_context
functions.description: Override code-variable ignore patterns for exceptions in the current context.

params.name: ignore_patterns
params.description: Variable-name patterns that should be omitted entirely         when code variables are captured.

functions.title: set_code_variables_mask_patterns_context
functions.description: Override code-variable mask patterns for exceptions in the current context.

params.name: mask_patterns
params.description: Variable-name patterns whose values should be replaced         with ``***`` when code variables are captured.

functions.title: set_code_variables_mask_url_credentials_context
functions.description: Whether to scrub credentials embedded in URLs/DSNs (e.g. user:pass@host) from captured code variables for the current context.

functions.title: set_context_device_id
functions.description: Set the device ID for the current context, associating all feature flag requests in this or child contexts with the given device ID.

params.name: device_id
params.description: The device ID to associate with the current context and its children

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import set_context_device_id
set_context_device_id("device_123")

functions.title: set_context_session
functions.description: Set the session ID for the current context.

params.name: session_id
params.description: The session ID to associate with the current context and its children

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import set_context_session
set_context_session("session_123")

functions.title: set_once
functions.description: Set properties on a user record, only if they do not yet exist.
functions.details: This will not overwrite previous people property values, unlike `set`. Otherwise, operates in an identical manner to `set`.

examples.name: Set property once
examples.code:
# Set property once
from posthog import set_once
set_once(distinct_id='distinct_id', properties={'initial_url': '/blog'})

functions.title: setup
functions.description: Create or return the global PostHog client configured by module settings.  Most applications should either instantiate ``Posthog`` directly or set ``posthog.api_key``/other module settings before calling top-level helpers. ``setup()`` is called automatically by global APIs such as ``capture()``.  Returns:     The global ``Client`` instance. If ``api_key`` is missing or blank,     the client is disabled and module-level calls become no-ops.

functions.title: shutdown
functions.description: Flush all messages and cleanly shutdown the client.

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import shutdown
shutdown()

functions.title: tag
functions.description: Add a tag to the current context.

params.name: name
params.description: The tag key

params.name: value
params.description: The tag value

examples.name: Example 1
examples.code:
from posthog import tag
tag("user_id", "123")
